speaker: MARTIUS
text: I’ll fight with none but thee, for I do hate thee

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Not Afric owns a serpent I abhor

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: More than thy fame and envy. Fix thy foot.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Let the first budger die the other’s slave,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: And the gods doom him after!

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Alone I fought in your Corioles’ walls

speaker: MARTIUS
text: And made what work I pleased. ’Tis not my blood

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Wherein thou seest me masked. For thy revenge,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Wrench up thy power to th’ highest.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: That was the whip of your bragged progeny,

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Thou shouldst not scape me here.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text:
(
                To
                 
                the
                 
                Volsces
                .
                )
                 
              Officious and not valiant, you have shamed me
